scope.summary: Makes the local XE18/XE21/Free23 rig a credential-free, one-command lifecycle harness with an explicit safe fallback when local credentials cannot be discovered.
out_of_scope: Remote Oracle databases and OCI Autonomous DB.
A container whose SYS password was rotated after creation and is absent from Docker config; the harness fails before bootstrap or smoke unless the operator supplies the lane override.
Stopping containers that were already running before the invocation.

entry_points.name: Rig L1 local Docker command
entry_points.trace: A clean detached worktree at source.sha ran `bash scripts/rig/oracle_l1.sh run --log` with no credential variables. The JSON-line result passed readiness, driver bootstrap, and SELECT 1 FROM dual for xe18, xe21, and free23. Two subsequent `down --log` commands both passed and the three pre-existing containers remained running. `rig_l1_dry_run_is_a_single_command_with_complete_lane_plan` also passed (1 passed) and asserts all three lane plans plus the no-credential command and pipefail-safe readiness check.

limitations: The local container run is integration evidence only: no committed exact-SHA live-evidence artifact was produced, so this close does not claim live-evidence readiness.
The lab containers were already running, so teardown was proved as an idempotent safe no-op twice; this close does not claim to have stopped and restarted a container owned by another process.
The full workspace quality gate was not run for this source commit.
